draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners want performance metrics to appear gradually during their run rather than pop instantly on screen, creating flow and reducing visual interruption.
facade.label: Run stats fade in
facade.acceptImplies: User values smooth transitions and progressive disclosure for real-time data during active movement.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immediate, snappy data readiness and finds gradual reveals slow or distracting while running.

facade.hypothesis: Runners want immediate pace/distance visibility; secondary context (temperature, UV) can be minimal
facade.label: Big number, small text
facade.acceptImplies: User values glanceable primary stats; support large-format display of 1-2 active metrics during run
facade.rejectImplies: User wants balanced information hierarchy; all metrics deserve equal visual weight or narrative context preferred over raw numbers

evidence.hypothesis: Runners want performance metrics to appear gradually during their run rather than pop instantly on screen, creating flow and reducing visual interruption.
evidence.implication: User values smooth transitions and progressive disclosure for real-time data during active movement.

facade.hypothesis: A playful, conversational tone with humor and personality will resonate better with runners than neutral, corporate language.
facade.label: Energetic and witty
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer personality and humor in their running app. Voice should feel like a friend, not a manual. Lean into puns, casual language, celebratory tone.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer straightforward, efficient, no-nonsense communication. Voice should stay neutral and professional. Skip the jokes; just give them the data they need fast.

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for core navigation (location, saved routes) paired with horizontal tabs beneath the header for secondary views (forecast, splits, recovery).
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: sidebar-first vs tabs-first vs mixed hierarchy
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs below
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to saved routes and location switching; willing to trade vertical space for persistent context.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers all navigation at top or in bottom tab bar; finds left sidebar wasteful for mobile/running context; wants maximum map/run display area.

facade.hypothesis: For a running weather app, users respond well to soft, warm lighting and rounded forms that feel approachable rather than clinical — this creates a sense of comfort and encouragement during physical exertion.
facade.axisTargeted: Light character (warm vs cool) and shape softness (rounded vs sharp)
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Explore golden hour palettes, soft gradients, rounded corners, and inviting color temperatures — suggests users want the app to feel motivational and human-scaled.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer cooler, crisper aesthetics with sharper geometry — the app should feel minimal, precise, and data-focused rather than warm and enveloping.

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from approachable, human warmth in its visual language—soft gradients, friendly typography, gentle color transitions—that feels encouraging rather than clinical or data-heavy.
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward friendly, gradient-based UI with soft edges, encouraging tone, and humanized data presentation (e.g., 'feels like a breeze' vs raw wind speed).
facade.rejectImplies: Explore cooler, more minimal or technical aesthetic—clean typography, high contrast, direct data display, sharper edges; appeals to efficiency-focused runners who want information fast.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. straightforward, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, reassuring interface vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity feel) vs. standard weather data
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, immediate feedback when checking conditions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. straightforward, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, reassuring interface vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity feel) vs. standard weather data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, immediate feedback when checking conditions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer metrics that animate into sharp focus during a glance rather than remaining static, creating a moment of attention-grabbing clarity that feels satisfying without requiring sustained focus.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and behavior — whether numeric data settles into place (kinetic arrival) vs. appears instantly (no transition)
facade.label: Numbers blur then snap
facade.acceptImplies: Runner interfaces should use entrance animations for critical metrics, treating number reveals as micro-moments of feedback that reward quick checks during movement.
facade.rejectImplies: Static number presentation is sufficient; runners don't benefit from or may find distracting the added motion of metrics animating into view.

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA
62 °F
Partly Cloudy
Wind
8 mph
Humidity
65%
Feels Like
58°F
UV Index
3

Next 6 Hours

Now
62°
Cloudy
2 PM
64°
Cloudy
4 PM
63°
Cloudy
6 PM
60°
Clear

Good Conditions for Running

Mild temps, light wind. Perfect for a steady pace.
